title: Provenance gate in CI (claims cited + datasets licensed)
context: The stats-for-clinicians project requires that every conceptual claim in every explainer has a resolvable sourceRef to an authoritative methods source, and that every example dataset is recorded in the data manifest with a permitted open license. These requirements are stated in the authoring template (template-002) and the data gate (datagate-003), but without automated enforcement they could be bypassed — an explainer with an uncited claim or an unlicensed dataset could merge. The provenance gate is a CI check that verifies these two constraints automatically, making provenance integrity a structural property of the build rather than a reviewer-dependent manual check.
objective: Build and merge a CI provenance gate that verifies (1) every conceptual claim in each explainer has a resolvable sourceRef and (2) every example dataset is recorded in the data manifest with a permitted license — and fails the build on any uncited claim or unlicensed/unrecorded dataset.
acceptanceCriteria: The provenance gate CI check verifies that every claims[] entry in every published/draft explainer has a non-empty sourceRef; build fails with a specific error if any claim has no sourceRef.

The gate verifies that every workedExample.datasetRef in every explainer corresponds to an entry in the data manifest (from datagate-003) with a license recorded and a redistribution-permitted status; build fails if any dataset is not in the manifest or has a missing/excluded license.
The gate also checks that every workedExample.scriptRef points to a committed file in the repo; build fails on a broken scriptRef.
The gate is MIT-licensed; CI is green on the existing project state (all existing explainers, if any, pass the gate).
The gate includes at least one golden test with a known-valid explainer and one with a known-invalid explainer (missing sourceRef / missing manifest entry) to verify the gate's own logic.
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint pass; commit DCO signed-off.
The gate is documented in the project contributing guide so authors know what will fail CI before opening a PR.
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-datagate-003.json — data manifest schema and PASS/EXCLUDE criterion
output: A merged PR containing the CI provenance gate; the gate verifies every claim has a sourceRef and every dataset is in the manifest with a permitted license, failing the build on any violation. MIT-licensed. CI green.
